prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `878187ab`.
codex_response:
Implemented `878187ab` end-to-end. The rule is: count the two non-7 colors, set `height=min(counts)` and `width=max(counts)`, then produce a fresh `16x16` grid of 7s with a bottom-left `height x width` block of 2s and mirrored 4-diagonals running inward from the bottom corners. I used the Opus hint as a starting hypothesis, but discarded the Sonnet hint and documented why in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_878187ab/notes.md).

Validation passed: official examples `3/3`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/878187ab/previews/preview_01.png). No new primitive was needed; `arc2/dsl_ext.py` was not changed.

The generator matches the task family by sampling sparse singleton scatter on `15x15` or `16x16` orange inputs, with count ranges tightened to the observed official regime so the resulting bottom-left rectangles and diagonal patterns stay on-distribution.
